name: Cochran County Schools
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union serving Cochran County, TX since 1961, offering savings accounts, certificates, vehicle loans, and personal lending with a not-for-profit service model.
description_long:
Cochran County Schools Federal Credit Union was established in 1961 by educators in Cochran County to provide affordable savings and lending services to school employees and community members. The organization operates on a member-owned cooperative model with the motto 'Not for profit, not for charity, but for service,' meaning earnings are returned to members through better rates and fewer fees rather than shareholders.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial products including Share Savings accounts (2.750% APY), Share Certificates with terms from 6 to 24 months (3.650%-3.750% APY), and multiple loan types including new and used vehicle loans, motorcycle loans, RV loans, personal loans, and equipment financing. They accept debit card payments by phone and online, provide car-buying services with an inventory of vehicles for sale, and offer free insurance quotes. Membership is open to anyone who lives, works, or worships in Cochran County, plus employees of Morton ISD, Whiteface CISD, Cochran Memorial Hospital, or Cochran County government.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its 63-year history of local community service, direct board representation by member-elected directors, and transparent fee structure. They maintain convenient hours (Monday-Friday, 9 a.m.-5 p.m.) and a physical location at 118 SW 1st, Morton, TX. The organization prioritizes accessibility with low minimum deposits ($50 for savings accounts) and one-time new account fees ($10).

As a small, community-focused credit union, Cochran County Schools FCU offers competitive rates and personalized service but may have limitations in digital banking infrastructure and product variety compared to larger financial institutions. The $10 convenience fee for online debit card payments and the 2-3 business day processing time for online payments are notable drawbacks. Prospective members should confirm membership eligibility based on residence, employment, or worship location in Cochran County.
services: Share Savings accounts with 2.750% APY dividends
Online and phone debit card payment processing
In-person account opening and member services
Board of director representation for members
pros: Member-owned cooperative returns profits through better rates and lower fees rather than shareholder distributions
Competitive dividend rates on savings (2.750% APY) and certificates (up to 3.750% APY)
Low account minimums ($50 initial deposit) and reasonable one-time new account fee ($10)
Diverse loan products including vehicle, motorcycle, RV, personal, equipment, and appliance loans
63-year track record serving Cochran County educators and community since 1961
Membership eligibility extended beyond school employees to anyone who lives, works, or worships in Cochran County
Now accepts online and phone debit card payments (Visa, MasterCard, Discover)
Free insurance quote services and car-buying assistance with inventory available
cons: $10 convenience fee charged for online debit card payments reduces accessibility
Limited to 2-3 business day processing time for online payments vs. instant processing at larger banks
Geographic restriction to Cochran County membership field limits accessibility for non-residents
Likely limited digital banking features and mobile app functionality vs. major financial institutions
Minimal information about alternative payment options online; requires in-person visit for fee-free options
best_for: Cochran County educators and school district employees seeking community-focused, member-owned financial services
Residents and workers in Cochran County, TX looking for competitive savings rates and lower lending costs
Vehicle buyers interested in combined financing and car-shopping services from a local lender
Community members seeking personal or equipment loans with relationship-based underwriting
diagnosis: Cochran County Schools FCU is best for Cochran County residents, employees, and those who worship in the county seeking a traditional member-owned credit union with competitive rates and personalized service. The main caveat is geographic restriction to Cochran County membership eligibility and limited digital banking infrastructure compared to larger banks—this is ideal for local community members but not accessible to the broader population.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
